jobs.title: Water Quality Analyst Level 3
jobs.location: Melrose Park, New South Wales, Australia
jobs.description:
35h full-time opportunity for a Water Quality Analyst Level 3
Based out of Sydney Water West Ryde laboratories
EA Level 7A $91,691.54 + Super 

About us

At Sydney Water, we’re passionate about making a difference to the lives of our customers and communities, and our employees. We have a proud heritage and a progressive future, providing world class drinking water and beautiful waterways from the mountains to the sea. Our people and their jobs are as diverse as the communities we serve.  

About the role

This role will work within the Trace Analysis team and will support the Sample Management team as required in Sydney Water’s world class laboratories and Australia’s largest dedicated water testing and research facilities. Help us innovate to meet the challenges of our growing cities by:

Demonstrating advanced experience in LC-MS/MS and/or GC-MS/MS, including method optimisation, instrument maintenance, troubleshooting, and complex problem resolution.
Applying a strong understanding of quality assurance and quality control principles for the analysis of water and wastewater, including compliance with NATA ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation requirements and ISO 9001 quality management systems.
Identifying, evaluating, and implementing opportunities to optimise laboratory assets, equipment, systems, and processes to improve efficiency, quality, and service delivery.
Performing laboratory sample preparation and analysis using specialised instrumentation, while consistently delivering accurate and timely results within agreed customer turnaround times.
Providing technical guidance, training, and mentoring to support capability development and knowledge sharing across the team.
Demonstrating proficiency in laboratory and business software systems, including Laboratory Information Management Systems (LIMS), scheduling tools, and Microsoft Office applications.
Promoting a strong safety culture through active compliance with health, safety, and environmental requirements, and taking personal responsibility for the safety and wellbeing of yourself and others.

About You

This role is suited to an experienced laboratory professional with a strong background in chemistry, environmental science, or chemical engineering, and extensive experience operating within a quality-focused laboratory environment. You are a proactive and collaborative team member who demonstrates technical expertise, sound judgement, and a commitment to safety, quality, and continuous improvement.

To be successful in this role, you will bring:

A relevant tertiary qualification (Degree and/or equivalent Certificate in Science) and demonstrated experience within a related scientific discipline
More than five years' experience working independently in a laboratory, with the ability to manage competing priorities and deliver high quality results.
Demonstrated experience performing complex analytical testing using advanced instrumentation such as LC-MS/MS and/or GC-MS/MS, including troubleshooting and routine maintenance.
Experience in PFAS analysis, including sample preparation, instrumental analysis, interpretation of results, and quality control processes, will be highly regarded.
Approval as an authorised signatory across multiple analytical methods, with the ability to review, validate, and release analytical data.
Experience working within a NATA-accredited laboratory, with a strong understanding of ISO/IEC 17025 requirements, quality assurance principles, and laboratory quality control systems.
A demonstrated ability to identify, lead, and implement continuous improvement initiatives, delivering measurable benefits in quality, efficiency, safety, or customer outcomes.
Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to share technical knowledge, support colleagues, and contribute positively to team performance.
A commitment to maintaining a safe working environment by consistently applying health, safety, and environmental requirements and promoting safe laboratory practices.
Strong collaboration skills, with the ability to provide technical guidance, share knowledge, and support the development of colleagues across the team.
Flexibility and a willingness to work outside core business hours, when required, to meet operational and contractual obligations.

Why Sydney Water?

Make a Real Impact: Your work keeps Sydney’s water flowing and our environment protected.
Work with the Best: Join a team that values safety, innovation, and looking out for each other.
Grow Your Career: Access training, development, and opportunities to advance.
Be Part of Something Bigger: Help deliver essential services to millions and safeguard Sydney’s future.

Please see the Position Description for the role’s full accountabilities.

Interested in this role?
 Apply now. Closing Date:  Wednesday 22nd July 2026.

You must be an Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident at the time of submitting your application to be considered for this role. Please note as part of the application process, you will be asked to undergo background checks which includes employment reference checks, a criminal history check and a medical assessment either online or in person.

Please advise if you require any adjustments to actively participate in the application and recruitment process. Please contact lorena.montes@sydneywater.com.au for any further information.

Dive into Our Great Benefits!

Competitive Compensation: 
We’ll ensure that your skills and contributions are valued and rewarded appropriately.  
Continuous Professional Growth: 
We will invest in your career with leadership and development programs to propel your success.
24/7 Wellbeing Support:
 Access for you and your family to free and confidential mental health and general health support through our EAP portal.
Discounted Private Health Insurance: 
Unlock affordable access to top-tier health coverage with our discounted insurance plans.
Family First:
 Prioritise your family with up to 18 weeks of fully paid parental leave and a generous childcare allowance.
Fitness Passport: 
Achieve your physical fitness goals using our Fitness Passport, granting you access to a multitude of gyms and pools across NSW. 
Novated Leasing: 
Get in the driver's seat of your financial future with our novated lease options.

As part of the application process, Sydney Water will be collecting your personal information to assess your suitability for the role. Your personal information may also be used to establish a Dayforce talent profile.

Your personal information will be collected in accordance with Dayforce’s Terms and Privacy Policy and Sydney Water’s Privacy Collection Statement – recruitment. Please note as part of the application process, you will be asked to undergo background checks which include a criminal history check.
